rationale: The primary residence at this Saratoga Springs, UT address shows a complex multi-gable/hip roof whose ridge is noticeably narrower than the overall building footprint, indicating a meaningful rise-to-run ratio. Eave shadows cast onto the ground below confirm meaningful roof elevation, ruling out shallow pitches (3:12–4:12). Multiple intersecting gable planes are distinctly visible from overhead with clear light-shadow contrast, consistent with moderate-to-steep slopes. The northern Utah climate (snow load requirements) and typical 2000s–2010s suburban construction style both support a 6:12 pitch. A 5:12 or 7:12 cannot be fully ruled out, but 6:12 best reconciles the ridge-to-footprint ratio, shadow length, and regional building norms.
